Docs.summary: Provides an HTTP-specific implementation of the class.
Initializes a new instance of the class. These constructors are obsolete.

Public ReadOnly Property Address As Uri
member this.Address : Uri
public: property Uri ^ Address { Uri ^ get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the Internet resource that actually responds to the request.
Docs.value: A that identifies the Internet resource that actually responds to the request. The default is the URI used by the method to initialize the request.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool AllowAutoRedirect { get; set; }
.property instance bool AllowAutoRedirect
Public Overridable Property AllowAutoRedirect As Boolean
member this.AllowAutoRedirect : bool with get, set
public: virtual property bool AllowAutoRedirect { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the request should follow redirection responses.
Docs.value: if the request should automatically follow redirection responses from the Internet resource; otherwise, . The default value is .

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool AllowReadStreamBuffering { get; set; }
.property instance bool AllowReadStreamBuffering
Public Overridable Property AllowReadStreamBuffering As Boolean
member this.AllowReadStreamBuffering : bool with get, set
public: virtual property bool AllowReadStreamBuffering { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether to buffer the received from the Internet resource.
Docs.value: to enable buffering of the data received from the Internet resource; to disable buffering. The default is .

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool AllowWriteStreamBuffering { get; set; }
.property instance bool AllowWriteStreamBuffering
Public Overridable Property AllowWriteStreamBuffering As Boolean
member this.AllowWriteStreamBuffering : bool with get, set
public: virtual property bool AllowWriteStreamBuffering { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether to buffer the data sent to the Internet resource.
Docs.value: to enable buffering of the data sent to the Internet resource; to disable buffering. The default is .

block.para: Setting to might cause performance problems when uploading large datasets because the data buffer could use all available memory.

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the type of decompression that is used.
Docs.value: A object that indicates the type of decompression that is used.
Docs.exception: The object's current state does not allow this property to be set.

The state object for this request.
Docs.summary: Begins an asynchronous request for a object to use to write data.
Docs.returns: An that references the asynchronous request.
Docs.exception:
The property is GET or HEAD.
-or-
is , is , is -1, is , and is POST or PUT.

The stream is being used by a previous call to
-or-
is set to a value and is .
-or-
The thread pool is running out of threads.

The request cache validator indicated that the response for this request can be served from the cache; however, requests that write data must not use the cache. This exception can occur if you are using a custom cache validator that is incorrectly implemented.
was previously called.

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the value of the HTTP header.
Docs.value: The value of the HTTP header. The default value is .
Docs.exception: The value of is set to Keep-alive or Close.

MemberSignature.Value: public override string? ConnectionGroupName { get; set; }
.property instance string ConnectionGroupName
Public Overrides Property ConnectionGroupName As String
member this.ConnectionGroupName : string with get, set
public: virtual property System::String ^ ConnectionGroupName { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };
public override string ConnectionGroupName { get; set; }

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the name of the connection group for the request.
Docs.value: The name of the connection group for this request. The default value is .

block.para: Each connection group creates additional connections for a server. This may result in exceeding the number of connections set by the property for that server.

MemberSignature.Value: public override long ContentLength { get; set; }
.property instance int64 ContentLength
Public Overrides Property ContentLength As Long
member this.ContentLength : int64 with get, set
public: virtual property long ContentLength { long get(); void set(long value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the HTTP header.
Docs.value: The number of bytes of data to send to the Internet resource. The default is -1, which indicates the property has not been set and that there is no request data to send.
Docs.exception: The request has been started by calling the , , , or method.
The new value is less than 0.

public System.Net.HttpContinueDelegate ContinueDelegate { get; set; }

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the delegate method called when an HTTP 100-continue response is received from the Internet resource.
Docs.value: A delegate that implements the callback method that executes when an HTTP Continue response is returned from the Internet resource. The default value is .

.property instance int32 ContinueTimeout
Public Property ContinueTimeout As Integer
member this.ContinueTimeout : int with get, set
public: property int ContinueTimeout { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a timeout, in milliseconds, to wait until the 100-Continue is received from the server.
Docs.value: The timeout, in milliseconds, to wait until the 100-Continue is received.

MemberSignature.Value: public static int DefaultMaximumErrorResponseLength { get; set; }
.property int32 DefaultMaximumErrorResponseLength
Public Shared Property DefaultMaximumErrorResponseLength As Integer
static member DefaultMaximumErrorResponseLength : int with get, set
public: static property int DefaultMaximumErrorResponseLength { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the default maximum length of an HTTP error response.
Docs.value: The default maximum length, in kilobytes (1024 bytes), of an HTTP error response.
Docs.exception: The value is less than 0 and is not equal to -1.

MemberSignature.Value: public static int DefaultMaximumResponseHeadersLength { get; set; }
.property int32 DefaultMaximumResponseHeadersLength
Public Shared Property DefaultMaximumResponseHeadersLength As Integer
static member DefaultMaximumResponseHeadersLength : int with get, set
public: static property int DefaultMaximumResponseHeadersLength { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the default for the property.
Docs.value: The length, in kilobytes (1024 bytes), of the default maximum for response headers received. The default configuration file sets this value to 64 kilobytes.
Docs.exception: The value is less than zero.

Docs.summary: Ends an asynchronous request for a object to use to write data.

MemberSignature.Value: public string? Expect { get; set; }
.property instance string Expect
Public Property Expect As String
member this.Expect : string with get, set
public: property System::String ^ Expect { System::String ^ get(); void set(System::String ^ value); };
public string Expect { get; set; }

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the value of the HTTP header.
Docs.value: The contents of the HTTP header. The default value is .
Docs.remarks: The value for this property is stored in . If WebHeaderCollection is set, the property value is lost.
Docs.exception: is set to a string that contains "100-continue" as a substring.

Gets a object to use to write request data.

The property is GET or HEAD.
-or-
is , is , is -1, is , and is POST or PUT.

The method is called more than once.
-or-
is set to a value and is .

The request cache validator indicated that the response for this request can be served from the cache; however, requests that write data must not use the cache. This exception can occur if you are using a custom cache validator that is incorrectly implemented.

was previously called.
-or-
The time-out period for the request expired.
-or-
An error occurred while processing the request.

The request cache validator indicated that the response for this request can be served from the cache; however, requests that write data must not use the cache. This exception can occur if you are using a custom cache validator that is incorrectly implemented.

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Net.WebResponse GetResponse ();
.method public hidebysig virtual instance class System.Net.WebResponse GetResponse() cil managed
Public Overrides Function GetResponse () As WebResponse
override this.GetResponse : unit -> System.Net.WebResponse
public: override System::Net::WebResponse ^ GetResponse();

Docs.summary: Returns a response from an Internet resource.
Docs.returns: A that contains the response from the Internet resource.
Docs.exception:
The stream is already in use by a previous call to .
-or-
is set to a value and is .

is GET or HEAD, and either is greater or equal to zero or is .
-or-
is , is , is -1, is , and is POST or PUT.
-or-
The has an entity body but the method is called without calling the method.
-or-
The is greater than zero, but the application does not write all of the promised data.

The request cache validator indicated that the response for this request can be served from the cache; however, this request includes data to be sent to the server. Requests that send data must not use the cache. This exception can occur if you are using a custom cache validator that is incorrectly implemented.

was previously called.
-or-
The time-out period for the request expired.
-or-
An error occurred while processing the request.

MemberSignature.Value: public virtual bool HaveResponse { get; }
.property instance bool HaveResponse
Public Overridable ReadOnly Property HaveResponse As Boolean
member this.HaveResponse : bool
public: virtual property bool HaveResponse { bool get(); };

Docs.summary: Gets a value that indicates whether a response has been received from an Internet resource.
Docs.value: if a response has been received; otherwise, .

remarks.format:
## Examples The following code example checks the <xref:System.Net.HttpWebRequest.HaveResponse> property to determine if a response has been received from an Internet resource.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Net/HttpWebRequest/HaveResponse/httpwebrequest_haveresponse.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Net/HttpWebRequest/HaveResponse/httpwebrequest_haveresponse.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public override System.Net.WebHeaderCollection Headers { get; set; }

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the Host header value to use in an HTTP request independent from the request URI.
Docs.value: The Host header value in the HTTP request.
Docs.exception: The Host header cannot be set to .
The Host header cannot be set to an invalid value.
The Host header cannot be set after the has already started to be sent.

Public Property KeepAlive As Boolean
member this.KeepAlive : bool with get, set
public: property bool KeepAlive { bool get(); void set(bool value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a value that indicates whether to make a persistent connection to the Internet resource.
Docs.value: if the request to the Internet resource should contain a HTTP header with the value Keep-alive; otherwise, . The default is .

MemberSignature.Value: public int MaximumAutomaticRedirections { get; set; }

Public Property MaximumAutomaticRedirections As Integer
member this.MaximumAutomaticRedirections : int with get, set
public: property int MaximumAutomaticRedirections { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of redirects that the request follows.
Docs.value: The maximum number of redirection responses that the request follows. The default value is 50.
Docs.exception: The value is set to 0 or less.

MemberSignature.Value: public int MaximumResponseHeadersLength { get; set; }

Public Property MaximumResponseHeadersLength As Integer
member this.MaximumResponseHeadersLength : int with get, set
public: property int MaximumResponseHeadersLength { int get(); void set(int value); };

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum allowed length of the response headers.
Docs.value: The length, in kilobytes (1024 bytes), of the response headers.
Docs.exception: The property is set after the request has already been submitted.
The value is less than 0.

Docs.value: The media type of the request. The default value is .

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the method for the request.
Docs.value: The request method to use to contact the Internet resource. The default value is GET.
Docs.exception:
No method is supplied.
-or-
The method string contains invalid characters.

Docs.summary: Gets or sets the version of HTTP to use for the request.
Docs.value: The HTTP version to use for the request. The default is .

Docs.value: The object to use to proxy the request. The default value is set by calling the property.

The caller does not have permission for the requested operation.

Docs.summary: Gets or sets a time-out in milliseconds when writing to or reading from a stream.
Docs.value: The number of milliseconds before the writing or reading times out. The default value is 300,000 milliseconds (5 minutes).

The value specified for a set operation is less than or equal to zero and is not equal to
